title: Five Tunnels Deep
synopsis: When Acorn gets separated from his group in the secret tunnels under the garden shed, he must find his way back by following mysterious clues in a countdown adventure through five magical tunnel chambers.

pages.text: The trapdoor clicks shut above, and suddenly the tunnel smells like rain and old wood and something else—something fizzy like magic. Acorn blinks his bright dark eyes and looks around. The other acorns are nowhere to be seen. Well, you might think this is a problem, but tunnels under garden sheds have their own rules. Things work differently down here. The walls glow with tiny mushrooms that give off blue light, and the floor is soft dirt mixed with lost buttons and forgotten marbles. Acorn notices five tunnels leading away from where he stands. Each one has a number carved into the earth above it. Five, four, three, two, one. 'Interesting,' he says out loud, because talking to yourself in magical tunnels is perfectly normal. His tiny stick arms brush against a spider web, and the spider waves hello with two legs while spinning with the other six. The living sentient acorn with his shiny brown cap decides to start with tunnel number five, since that seems like the most logical place to begin a countdown.

illustration.description: A living sentient acorn with a shiny brown cap, tiny stick arms, leaf feet, and bright dark eyes painted on the nut stands in an underground chamber with five tunnel entrances marked with glowing numbers, surrounded by tiny blue mushrooms, scattered buttons, lost marbles, spider webs with friendly spiders, tree roots, and magical sparkles in the air.
illustration.alt: Acorn in the main tunnel chamber with five numbered entrances

pages.text: Tunnel five leads to a room full of things that used to belong upstairs. Now, you should know that when things fall through the cracks of garden sheds, they don't just disappear—they come here. Old spoons stand in neat rows like silver soldiers. Bottle caps form spirals on the ground. A rubber ball bounces by itself in the corner, keeping time like a metronome. Acorn's leaf feet make soft padding sounds as he walks between towers of lost socks. 'Has anyone seen a group of acorns?' he calls out. The rubber ball bounces twice for no, then keeps bouncing. A pair of reading glasses perched on a small rock adjust themselves to look at him better. 'They went through the four tunnel about ten minutes ago,' the glasses say. Their voice sounds like someone reading a very interesting book. 'Thank you,' says Acorn politely, because magical creatures always remember their manners. He heads toward the exit marked with a glowing four, stepping carefully around a colony of ants carrying a lost earring toward their hill.

illustration.description: A living sentient acorn with a shiny brown cap, tiny stick arms, leaf feet, and bright dark eyes painted on the nut explores a cluttered underground room filled with towers of lost socks, rows of old spoons, spiral patterns of bottle caps, a bouncing rubber ball, reading glasses on rocks, ants carrying jewelry, and various forgotten household objects.
illustration.alt: Acorn in the lost objects chamber

pages.text: The four tunnel slopes downward and opens into a library made entirely of leaves. Thousands and thousands of leaves hang from the ceiling on invisible threads, and each one has writing on it in gold letters. When wind blows through (and there's always wind in leaf libraries), the leaves whisper their stories to each other. 'Excuse me,' Acorn says to a particularly large maple leaf that's swaying nearby. 'I'm looking for my friends.' The maple leaf rustles thoughtfully. 'Check the card catalog,' it suggests, pointing with its stem toward a small wooden box sitting on a mushroom. The living sentient acorn with his shiny brown cap walks over and opens the box. Inside are tiny cards made of flower petals, organized by scent instead of alphabet. He finds the card that smells like other acorns—nutty and woody and friendly. The card reads: 'Your group stopped here to listen to a story about brave seeds. They left through the three tunnel when the story ended.' A gentle oak leaf floats down and taps him on his shiny brown cap. 'The story was about you, little one,' it whispers. 'You're braver than you know.'

illustration.description: A living sentient acorn with a shiny brown cap, tiny stick arms, leaf feet, and bright dark eyes painted on the nut stands in an underground library where thousands of leaves hang from the ceiling with golden writing, a card catalog made of flower petals sits on a mushroom, and floating leaves create a magical reading atmosphere with swirling words and letters in the air.
illustration.alt: Acorn in the magical leaf library

pages.text: Tunnel three winds like a snake and leads to the most surprising place yet—a room full of screens. But these aren't the glowing rectangles you might be thinking of. These screens are made of crystallized water, and they show memories instead of movies. Acorn sees himself playing with his friends yesterday, rolling down hills and playing hide-and-seek among the flower pots. Another screen shows his mother teaching him how to dig proper holes for planting. A third screen displays his whole group learning about the secret tunnels for the first time. The living sentient acorn with his shiny brown cap realizes something important: all the best memories are about being with others, not staring at screens. A wise old badger sits in the corner, polishing the crystal screens with a soft cloth. 'They're prettier when they're not being watched all the time,' the badger mentions casually. 'Your friends went to tunnel two. They said to tell you they're proud of how you're finding your own way.' The badger hands Acorn a small crystal. 'For luck,' he says. 'And for remembering what matters most.'

illustration.description: A living sentient acorn with a shiny brown cap, tiny stick arms, leaf feet, and bright dark eyes painted on the nut stands among crystalline memory screens showing various scenes, with a wise old badger polishing crystals, magical reflections, floating memory bubbles, scattered crystal fragments, and soft glowing light emanating from the crystal surfaces.
illustration.alt: Acorn in the crystal memory chamber with the badger

pages.text: The two tunnel is shorter than the others and opens into a cozy room that smells like home. There they are—all of Acorn's friends, sitting in a circle and sharing stories about their own tunnel adventures. When they see him, they cheer and make room in the circle. 'We knew you'd find us,' says his best friend, a acorn named Hazel. 'We left clues everywhere we went.' 'I followed them all,' Acorn says proudly, settling down with his leaf feet tucked under him. 'And I learned something important. The screens down here are different from the ones up there. These ones show real memories, real connections.' An elderly acorn with a cap covered in moss nods approvingly. 'The one tunnel leads back to the beginning,' she explains. 'But first, we always gather here to share what we've discovered. Every adventure teaches us something about ourselves.' The living sentient acorn with his shiny brown cap looks around at his friends' faces, golden in the soft tunnel light. He thinks about his journey through five, four, three, two, and realizes that one—being together—is the most magical number of all. Above them, the garden shed creaks gently, and somewhere up there, the real world keeps spinning. But down here in tunnel one, surrounded by friends and stories and the sweet smell of earth, Acorn knows he's exactly where he belongs.

illustration.description: A living sentient acorn with a shiny brown cap, tiny stick arms, leaf feet, and bright dark eyes painted on the nut sits in a circle with other similar acorns in a cozy underground chamber filled with soft moss cushions, glowing fungi providing warm light, scattered crystals and tokens from their journey, roots forming natural seats, and tunnel entrances marked with numbers in the background.
illustration.alt: Acorn reunited with friends in the final tunnel chamber

coverPrompt.description: A living sentient acorn with a shiny brown cap, tiny stick arms, leaf feet, and bright dark eyes painted on the nut stands at the entrance to a mysterious tunnel filled with glowing objects, old tools, scattered seeds, forgotten toys, crystal formations, and winding pathways disappearing into darkness.
coverPrompt.alt: Acorn at the entrance to mysterious tunnels

questions.question: How many tunnels did Acorn see at the beginning?
questions.explanation: Acorn saw five tunnels numbered five, four, three, two, and one.

questions.question: What made the walls glow in the tunnels?
questions.explanation: The walls glowed with tiny mushrooms that gave off blue light.

questions.question: What was special about the leaf library?
choices: Leaves had golden writing and whispered stories
questions.explanation: The leaves had writing in gold letters and whispered their stories to each other.

questions.question: What did the crystal screens show?
questions.explanation: The crystal screens showed memories instead of movies, like Acorn playing with friends.

questions.question: Where did Acorn find his friends?
questions.explanation: Acorn found his friends in tunnel one, where they were sharing stories in a circle.
